scenarios.title: Saying You Feel Overwhelmed
scenarios.description: Practice telling someone when you have too much going on.

scenarios.title: Talking About Feeling Left Out
scenarios.description: Practice speaking up when you feel excluded.

scenarios.title: Asking Someone to Forgive You
scenarios.description: Practice giving a genuine apology and asking for forgiveness.

scenarios.title: Forgiving a Friend
scenarios.description: Practice accepting an apology and choosing to move forward.

scenarios.title: Trying Something New
scenarios.description: Practice talking about nervousness and building courage to try new things.

scenarios.title: Buying Something at a Store
scenarios.description: Practice finding items, paying, and being polite with store staff.

scenarios.title: At the Doctor
scenarios.description: Practice checking in, describing symptoms, and understanding the treatment plan.

scenarios.title: Making a Phone Call
scenarios.description: Practice introducing yourself on a call, stating your purpose, and saying goodbye.

scenarios.title: At the Dentist
scenarios.description: Practice checking in, describing discomfort, and asking questions.

scenarios.title: Asking the Librarian for Help
scenarios.description: Practice asking for help finding a book, checking it out, and being polite.

ow_a1_bridge.say: Let us practice what to do when everything feels like too much.

ow_a1_q1.say: How do you feel right now? Can you name your feeling?

ow_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. How do you feel right now? Can you name your feeling?

ow_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I feel really overwhelmed right now. Let's keep going.

ow_a1_q2.say: Good. Who could you tell when you feel overwhelmed?
ow_a1_q2.hint: Say: I could tell a teacher or friend

ow_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Good. Who could you tell when you feel overwhelmed?
ow_a1_q2_r.hint: Say: I could tell a teacher or friend

ow_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I could tell my teacher or a trusted friend. Let's keep going.

ow_a1_q3.say: What would you say to start the conversation?
ow_a1_q3.hint: Say: Excuse me, can I talk to you?

ow_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What would you say to start the conversation?
ow_a1_q3_r.hint: Say: Excuse me, can I talk to you?

ow_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me, can I talk to you for a moment. Let's keep going.

ow_a1_q4.say: How would you explain what is happening?
ow_a1_q4.hint: Say: I have a lot going on right now

ow_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. How would you explain what is happening?
ow_a1_q4_r.hint: Say: I have a lot going on right now

ow_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I have too much going on and I am struggling. Let's keep going.

ow_a1_q5.say: What kind of help could you ask for?
ow_a1_q5.hint: Say: Could I have a short break?

ow_a1_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What kind of help could you ask for?
ow_a1_q5_r.hint: Say: Could I have a short break?

ow_a1_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could I take a short break or talk to someone. Let's keep going.

ow_a2_bridge.say: You started the conversation. Now explain more.

ow_a2_q1.say: The adult says: Tell me more. What is making you feel this way?

ow_a2_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The adult says: Tell me more. What is making you feel this way?

ow_a2_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: My homework and the test on Friday are stressing me out. Let's keep going.

ow_a2_q2.say: They ask: Is there one thing that feels biggest right now?

ow_a2_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask: Is there one thing that feels biggest right now?

ow_a2_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: The test coming up feels the biggest right now. Let's keep going.

ow_a2_q3.say: They say: That sounds hard. How long have you felt this way?

ow_a2_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say: That sounds hard. How long have you felt this way?

ow_a2_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I have felt this way for a few days now. Let's keep going.

ow_a2_q4.say: They ask: Did you sleep okay last night?

ow_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask: Did you sleep okay last night?

ow_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: No I did not sleep very well last night. Let's keep going.

ow_a2_q5.say: They say: Thank you for telling me. How can I help?

ow_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say: Thank you for telling me. How can I help?

ow_a2_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: A short break and somewhere quiet would really help. Let's keep going.

ow_a3_bridge.say: They are helping you make a plan.

ow_a3_q1.say: They suggest: What if we break your tasks into smaller steps?

ow_a3_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They suggest: What if we break your tasks into smaller steps?

ow_a3_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes that sounds really helpful thank you. Let's keep going.

ow_a3_q2.say: They ask: Which task should we start with?

ow_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask: Which task should we start with?

ow_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Let us start with the easiest one first. Let's keep going.

ow_a3_q3.say: They say: Good choice. How much time do you need for that?

ow_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say: Good choice. How much time do you need for that?

ow_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Maybe about thirty minutes for that one. Let's keep going.

ow_a3_q4.say: They ask: Do you want someone to check in with you while you work?

ow_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask: Do you want someone to check in with you while you work?

ow_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes please that would help me stay on track. Let's keep going.

ow_a3_q5.say: They say: You can do this. What will you do first?

ow_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say: You can do this. What will you do first?

ow_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will sit down and open my notebook first. Let's keep going.

ow_a4_bridge.say: After a short break you feel a bit better.

ow_a4_q1.say: They check in: How are you feeling now?

ow_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They check in: How are you feeling now?

ow_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I feel a little better now thank you. Let's keep going.

ow_a4_q2.say: They ask: Did the break help?

ow_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask: Did the break help?

ow_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes it helped me calm down a bit. Let's keep going.

ow_a4_q3.say: They say: I am glad. Is there anything else you need?

ow_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say: I am glad. Is there anything else you need?

ow_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I think I am okay now but I may need help later. Let's keep going.

ow_a4_q4.say: They say: Remember you can always come to me. What will you do next time?

ow_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say: Remember you can always come to me. What will you do next time?

ow_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Next time I will ask for help sooner. Let's keep going.

ow_a4_q5.say: They say: Great plan. Anything else before you go back?

ow_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say: Great plan. Anything else before you go back?

ow_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: No thank you so much for listening and helping me. Let's keep going.

ow_a5_bridge.say: Let us practice some calming strategies.

ow_a5_q1.say: When you feel overwhelmed a deep breath helps. Can you say: I will take a deep breath?
ow_a5_q1.hint: Say: I will take a deep breath

ow_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. When you feel overwhelmed a deep breath helps. Can you say: I will take a deep breath?
ow_a5_q1_r.hint: Say: I will take a deep breath

ow_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will take a deep breath to calm down. Let's keep going.

ow_a5_q2.say: Another strategy is counting. Can you say: I will count to ten?
ow_a5_q2.hint: Say: I will count to ten

ow_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Another strategy is counting. Can you say: I will count to ten?
ow_a5_q2_r.hint: Say: I will count to ten

ow_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will count slowly to ten in my head. Let's keep going.

ow_a5_q3.say: You could also write it down. Can you say: I will write down how I feel?
ow_a5_q3.hint: Say: I will write it down

ow_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You could also write it down. Can you say: I will write down how I feel?
ow_a5_q3_r.hint: Say: I will write it down

ow_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will write down how I feel and why. Let's keep going.

ow_a5_q4.say: Talking to a friend can also help. Can you say: I will call a friend?
ow_a5_q4.hint: Say: I will talk to a friend

ow_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Talking to a friend can also help. Can you say: I will call a friend?
ow_a5_q4_r.hint: Say: I will talk to a friend

ow_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I will call or text a friend I trust. Let's keep going.

ow_a5_q5.say: Which strategy feels easiest for you?

ow_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Which strategy feels easiest for you?

ow_a5_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I think deep breathing feels easiest for me right now. Let's keep going.

ow_a6_bridge.say: Let us practice asking a teacher for a break.

ow_a6_q1.say: You walk up to your teacher. What do you say first?

ow_a6_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You walk up to your teacher. What do you say first?

ow_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me, do you have a second. Let's keep going.

ow_a6_q2.say: Teacher looks up. What do you say next?

ow_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Teacher looks up. What do you say next?

ow_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am feeling really overwhelmed and I need a moment. Let's keep going.

ow_a6_q3.say: Teacher says: Of course. What do you need?

ow_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Teacher says: Of course. What do you need?

ow_a6_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could I step outside for a quick break please. Let's keep going.

ow_a6_q4.say: Teacher says: Yes, five minutes. What do you say?

ow_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Teacher says: Yes, five minutes. What do you say?

ow_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you so much I really appreciate it. Let's keep going.

ow_a6_q5.say: After the break you return. What do you say?

ow_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. After the break you return. What do you say?

ow_a6_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am back and feeling much better now thank you. Let's keep going.

ow_a7_bridge.say: Let us talk about what you can do on your own.

ow_a7_q1.say: What is something you can do alone when you feel overwhelmed?

ow_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What is something you can do alone when you feel overwhelmed?

ow_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can sit somewhere quiet and breathe slowly. Let's keep going.

ow_a7_q2.say: How long should you try calming down before asking for help?

ow_a7_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. How long should you try calming down before asking for help?

ow_a7_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I should try for a few minutes first. Let's keep going.

ow_a7_q3.say: What if the calming strategy does not work?

ow_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What if the calming strategy does not work?

ow_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Then I should go and ask an adult for help. Let's keep going.

ow_a7_q4.say: Is it okay to ask for help when you feel overwhelmed?

ow_a7_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Is it okay to ask for help when you feel overwhelmed?

ow_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes it is always okay to ask for help. Let's keep going.

ow_a7_q5.say: Who is one person you could go to at school?

ow_a7_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Who is one person you could go to at school?

ow_a7_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would go to my counselor or homeroom teacher. Let's keep going.

ow_a8_bridge.say: Final practice: putting it all together.

ow_a8_q1.say: You feel overwhelmed in the hallway. What is your first step?

ow_a8_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You feel overwhelmed in the hallway. What is your first step?

ow_a8_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would stop and take a slow deep breath first. Let's keep going.

ow_a8_q2.say: Now you need to tell someone. What do you say?

ow_a8_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Now you need to tell someone. What do you say?

ow_a8_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me I am feeling overwhelmed and need some help. Let's keep going.

ow_a8_q3.say: They ask what happened. What do you say?

ow_a8_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask what happened. What do you say?

ow_a8_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I have a lot going on and it all hit me at once. Let's keep going.

ow_a8_q4.say: They offer to help. What do you say?

ow_a8_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They offer to help. What do you say?

ow_a8_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you yes please that would mean a lot. Let's keep going.

ow_a8_q5.say: After the talk how do you feel?

ow_a8_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. After the talk how do you feel?

ow_a8_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I feel calmer and better now thank you. Let's keep going.

ow_win.say: Amazing! You told someone you were overwhelmed and asked for help. That is so brave! You earned a star!

lo_a1_bridge.say: It can hurt to feel left out. Let us practice talking about it.

lo_a1_q1.say: How do you feel when you are not included?

lo_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. How do you feel when you are not included?

lo_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I feel really left out and a little hurt. Let's keep going.

lo_a1_q2.say: Who do you feel left out by?

lo_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Who do you feel left out by?

lo_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: My friends or the group I usually sit with. Let's keep going.

lo_a1_q3.say: How do you know you were left out?

lo_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. How do you know you were left out?

lo_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: They made plans and did not tell me or invite me. Let's keep going.

lo_a1_q4.say: How long have you felt this way?

lo_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. How long have you felt this way?

lo_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: It has been happening for a few days now. Let's keep going.

lo_a1_q5.say: Is there someone you could talk to about it?

lo_a1_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Is there someone you could talk to about it?

lo_a1_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I could talk to a trusted friend or my counselor. Let's keep going.

lo_a2_bridge.say: Let us practice talking to a friend about it.

lo_a2_q1.say: You see your friend. How do you start the conversation?

lo_a2_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You see your friend. How do you start the conversation?

lo_a2_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hey do you have a minute to talk. Let's keep going.

lo_a2_q2.say: Friend says sure. What do you say?

lo_a2_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Friend says sure. What do you say?

lo_a2_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I heard about the plans and felt left out because I was not invited. Let's keep going.

lo_a2_q3.say: Friend says they did not mean to. How do you respond?

lo_a2_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Friend says they did not mean to. How do you respond?

lo_a2_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I understand but it still hurt my feelings. Let's keep going.

lo_a2_q4.say: Friend apologizes. What do you say?

lo_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Friend apologizes. What do you say?

lo_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you for saying that it means a lot to me. Let's keep going.

lo_a2_q5.say: Friend asks to include you next time. What do you say?

lo_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Friend asks to include you next time. What do you say?

lo_a2_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes that would make me really happy thank you. Let's keep going.

lo_a3_bridge.say: Sometimes you need to ask to be included.

lo_a3_q1.say: Your group is making plans. How do you ask to join?

lo_a3_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Your group is making plans. How do you ask to join?

lo_a3_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hey that sounds fun can I come along. Let's keep going.

lo_a3_q2.say: They say they were not sure you wanted to come. What do you say?

lo_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say they were not sure you wanted to come. What do you say?

lo_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I definitely want to come I would love to be included. Let's keep going.

lo_a3_q3.say: They ask what time works for you. What do you say?

lo_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask what time works for you. What do you say?

lo_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am free on Saturday afternoon or after school. Let's keep going.

lo_a3_q4.say: They agree on a time. What do you say?

lo_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They agree on a time. What do you say?

lo_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Great I am excited to come thank you for including me. Let's keep going.

lo_a3_q5.say: Before you go how do you confirm the plan?

lo_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Before you go how do you confirm the plan?

lo_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: So we are meeting Saturday at three right. Let's keep going.

lo_a4_bridge.say: What if someone left you out on purpose?

lo_a4_q1.say: You think it was on purpose. What do you do first?

lo_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You think it was on purpose. What do you do first?

lo_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would take a breath and make sure I have the right information. Let's keep going.

lo_a4_q2.say: You decide to ask them directly. What do you say?

lo_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You decide to ask them directly. What do you say?

lo_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I felt left out and I wanted to ask if something is wrong. Let's keep going.

lo_a4_q3.say: They say nothing is wrong. How do you respond?

lo_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say nothing is wrong. How do you respond?

lo_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Okay I am glad nothing is wrong I just wanted to check. Let's keep going.

lo_a4_q4.say: But you still feel hurt. What do you do?

lo_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. But you still feel hurt. What do you do?

lo_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would still talk to a trusted adult about how I feel. Let's keep going.

lo_a4_q5.say: What would help you feel better going forward?

lo_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What would help you feel better going forward?

lo_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: It would help if people just let me know what is going on. Let's keep going.

lo_a5_bridge.say: Let us talk about feelings after being left out.

lo_a5_q1.say: It is normal to feel sad. Can you say: It is okay to feel sad sometimes?

lo_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. It is normal to feel sad. Can you say: It is okay to feel sad sometimes?

lo_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: It is okay and normal to feel sad sometimes. Let's keep going.

lo_a5_q2.say: What can you do when you feel sad about being left out?

lo_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What can you do when you feel sad about being left out?

lo_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can talk to a friend or write in my journal. Let's keep going.

lo_a5_q3.say: Is it okay to tell someone your feelings were hurt?

lo_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Is it okay to tell someone your feelings were hurt?

lo_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes it is always okay to say your feelings were hurt. Let's keep going.

lo_a5_q4.say: What if no one is available to talk right now?

lo_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What if no one is available to talk right now?

lo_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would write it down or listen to music and come back later. Let's keep going.

lo_a5_q5.say: After talking how do you usually feel?

lo_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. After talking how do you usually feel?

lo_a5_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I usually feel better and more relieved after talking about it. Let's keep going.

lo_a6_bridge.say: Let us practice with a counselor.

lo_a6_q1.say: You knock on the counselor door. What do you say?

lo_a6_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You knock on the counselor door. What do you say?

lo_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hi do you have a moment I need to talk about something. Let's keep going.

lo_a6_q2.say: Counselor says come in. What do you say?

lo_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Counselor says come in. What do you say?

lo_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I have been feeling left out by my friends lately and it has been hard. Let's keep going.

lo_a6_q3.say: They ask: How long has this been happening?

lo_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask: How long has this been happening?

lo_a6_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: It started about a week ago and has been bothering me. Let's keep going.

lo_a6_q4.say: They ask: Have you talked to your friends about it?

lo_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask: Have you talked to your friends about it?

lo_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Not yet I was not sure how to bring it up. Let's keep going.

lo_a6_q5.say: They offer to help you practice. What do you say?

lo_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They offer to help you practice. What do you say?

lo_a6_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes please that would really help me thank you. Let's keep going.

lo_a7_bridge.say: Preparing to talk to your friend group.

lo_a7_q1.say: You decide to speak up. What do you say to the group?

lo_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You decide to speak up. What do you say to the group?

lo_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hey I wanted to say something to you all if that is okay. Let's keep going.

lo_a7_q2.say: You explain you have been feeling left out. What words do you use?

lo_a7_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You explain you have been feeling left out. What words do you use?

lo_a7_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I have been feeling left out when plans are made without me. Let's keep going.

lo_a7_q3.say: A friend says sorry we did not realize. How do you respond?

lo_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. A friend says sorry we did not realize. How do you respond?

lo_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you for saying that I appreciate it. Let's keep going.

lo_a7_q4.say: You want to make it easier in the future. What do you suggest?

lo_a7_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You want to make it easier in the future. What do you suggest?

lo_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Maybe we could use a group chat to plan things together. Let's keep going.

lo_a7_q5.say: Everyone agrees. How do you close the conversation?

lo_a7_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Everyone agrees. How do you close the conversation?
